title: Sensor unit, body fluid monitoring device and method for detecting an analyte
abstract: Provided is a sensor unit (104) for detecting an analyte (128) in an aqueous medium. The sensor unit comprises a surface (124) for receiving the aqueous medium thereon. Capture species (126) immobilized on the surface reversibly bind the analyte. A detector (130) detects the analyte when the analyte is bound to the capture species. The sensor unit further comprises an electrolysis assembly (132). The electrolysis assembly comprises a plurality of spatially separated electrically conductive areas (134) on the surface, and a power supply (136) for supplying a voltage across at least two of the electrically conductive areas. The voltage is sufficient to electrolyse the aqueous medium received on the surface. Further provided is a body fluid monitoring device (101) comprising the sensor unit, and a method for detecting an analyte.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS: 1. A sensor unit (104) for detecting an analyte in an aqueous medium, the sensor unit comprising: a surface (124) for receiving the aqueous medium thereon; capture species (126) for reversibly binding said analyte, the capture species being immobilized on said surface; a detector (130) for detecting, determining or assessing analyte bound to said capture species; and an electrolysis assembly (132) comprising: at least three spatially separated electrically conductive areas (134) on said surface; and a power supply (136) configured to implement: a first setting in which a voltage sufficient to electrolyse the aqueous medium received on said surface is supplied across a first pairwise combination of said at least three conductive areas; and a second setting in which a voltage sufficient to electrolyse the aqueous medium received on said surface is supplied across a second pairwise combination of said at least three conductive areas, the second pairwise combination being different from said first pairwise combination. 2. The sensor unit (104) according to claim 1, wherein at least two of said electrically conductive areas (138, 140) are arranged relative to each other in an interdigitated configuration. 3. The sensor unit (104) according to claim 1 or claim 2, wherein the voltage supplied in the second setting is different from the voltage supplied in the first setting. 4. The sensor unit (104) according to any of claims 1 to 3, wherein the second pairwise combination of conductive areas are spaced further apart on said surface (124) than the first pairwise combination of conductive areas. 5. The sensor unit (104) according to any of claims 1 to 4, wherein the plurality of spatially separated electrically conductive areas (134) comprises an array of conductive areas in which the spacing between adjacent conductive areas alternates between a larger and a smaller separation, wherein said power supply (136) is configured to supply the voltage sufficient to electrolyse the aqueous medium across adjacent conductive areas spaced by said larger separation (158, 160, 162), the adjacent conductive areas spaced by said smaller separation being of the same polarity as each other. 6. The sensor unit (104) according to any of claims 1 to 5, wherein the plurality of spatially separated electrically conductive areas (134) comprises a grid of electrically conductive strips, the power supply (136) being configured to implement: a first mode in which the voltage sufficient to electrolyse the aqueous medium is supplied across parallel strips extending in a first direction; and a second mode in which the voltage sufficient to electrolyse the aqueous medium is supplied across parallel strips extending in a second direction, the second direction being different from said first direction, optionally wherein the power supply is configured to implement said first and second modes sequentially. 7. The sensor unit (104) according to any of claims 1 to 6, wherein the power supply (136) is configured to switch the polarity of the voltage across the electrically conductive areas (134). 8. The sensor unit (104) according to any of claims 1 to 7, wherein the detector (130) comprises a transducer, optionally wherein the detector comprises at least one of a quartz crystal resonator detector and a surface plasmon detector. 9. The sensor unit (104) according to claim 8, wherein said transducer is arranged to transmit and/or receive electrical signals via at least some of said spatially separated electrically conductive areas (134). 10. The sensor unit (104) according to any of claims 1 to 9, wherein the conductive areas (134) comprise an optically transmissive material. 11. The sensor unit (104) according to any of claims 1 to 10, comprising a controller (142) configured to trigger the electrolysis assembly (132) to electrolyse said aqueous medium in response to a trigger signal; optionally wherein controller comprises a clock module (168) configured to send a clock signal (169) to the controller at predetermined intervals, the trigger signal comprising said clock signal. 12. The sensor unit (104) according to claim 11, wherein the controller (142) comprises a sensor signal variation module (170) configured to generate a variation signal (171) in response to a signal (175) received from the detector (130), the trigger signal comprising said variation signal; optionally wherein the signal variation module is configured to generate said variation signal in response to saturation of the detector. 13. The sensor unit (104) according to claim 11 or claim 12, comprising a further sensor (172) for monitoring a body parameter, said further sensor being configured to generate an event signal (173) in response to a change in said body parameter, wherein the trigger signal comprises said event signal. 14. A body fluid monitoring device (101) for detecting an analyte in an aqueous medium produced by the body, the device comprising: the sensor unit (104) according to any of claims 1 to 13; and a fluid collection assembly (102) for supplying said aqueous medium to the surface (124) of said sensor unit; optionally wherein the device is a sweat monitoring device. 15. A method (200) for detecting an analyte in an aqueous medium, the method comprising: providing (202) capture species immobilized on a surface; receiving (204) said aqueous medium on the surface such that the analyte binds to said capture species; detecting, determining or assessing (206) the bound analyte; and releasing (208) the detected analyte from the capture species by electrolysing the aqueous medium received on said surface, wherein the electrolysing comprises supplying a voltage sufficient to electrolyse the aqueous medium across a first pairwise combination of at least three conductive areas, and supplying a voltage sufficient to electrolyse the aqueous 

 medium across a second pairwise combination of the at least three conductive areas, the second pairwise combination being different from the first pairwise combination.
